title: Solvent heating system for preparative separations
abstract: The present invention is directed to a mobile phase preheater (3) for a medium pressure liquid chromatograph (MPLC) where high flow rates, of at least 10mL/min, are used. The preheater comprises a stainless steel tube, which is directly heated by application of a voltage across the tube (Joule effect). The preheater is loocated upstream of the injector (4).
claims:
Claims 1. A preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph (MPLC) adapted for a flow rate of at least 10 ml/min comprising : a chromatographic column (6) having: an inlet port (16) for receiving a mobile phase and a sample, and, an effluent port (15) for discharging the mobile phase and separated components of the sample from said chromatographic column, a binary pump system ( 1 ) , an injection system (4) for injecting the sample in a mobile phase, a solvent tubing assembly (2), and wherein the MPLC comprises a mobile phase heating device (3) connected to said solvent tubing assembly (2) upstream the injection system (4) of said chromatographic column (6), said mobile phase heating device (3) comprising: a stainless steel tube (13) at least one heater system (14) for the power supply, generating heat by the Joule effect by applying electric current directly on said stainless steel tube (13), a thermostat controller (12) capable of stabilizing the temperature of the mobile phase by switching said at least one heater system (14) on or off, and characterized in that said mobile phase heating device (3) presents a thermal inertia of less than one minute at a flow rate of 40 ml/min for a mobile phase temperature of 25Â°C at the inlet of the mobile phase heating device (3) and stabilized at 45 Â°C at the outlet of said mobile phase heating device (3). 2. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph of claim 1, wherein the thermostat controller (12) is controlled by a computer program product (11) comprising computer-executable instructions embodied in a computer-readable medium for controlling said mobile phase heating device. 3. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph according to any one of claims 1-2, wherein said mobile phase heating device (3) further comprises an over temperature sensor (15) capable of monitoring the temperature of the mobile phase. 4. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph according to any one of claims 1-3, wherein the measured temperature of the mobile phase can be stabilized with a precision of 0.1 Â°C through the thermostat controller (12). 5. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph according to any one of claims 1-4, wherein the flow rates carried out by the mobile phase heating device (3) is comprised between 10 to 100 ml/min. 6. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph according to any one of claims 1-5, further comprising a pressure detector (5) placed between the injection system (4) and said chromatographic column (6) so as to monitor the pressure at the inlet port (16) of the column (6). 7. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph of claim 6, wherein the pressure detector (5) is an electronic pressure gauge. 8. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph of any one of claims 6 to 7, wherein the pressure detector is connected to a computer adapted to control the pressure (11). 9. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the stainless steel tube (13) includes tubing in the form of a serpentine loop. 10. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the chromatography column (6) is heated by a column heater (7). 11. The preparative medium pressure liquid chromatograph of claim 10, wherein the column heater (7) is a thermostatically oven or a heating tape. 12. A MPLC mobile phase heating device (3) adapted to be connected to a solvent tubing assembly (2) upstream the injection system (4) of a chromatographic column (6), wherein said mobile phase heating device (3) comprises: a stainless steel tube (13) at least one heater system (14) for the power supply, generating heat by the Joule effect by applying electric current directly on said stainless steel tube (13), 

 a thermostat controller (12) capable of stabilizing the temperature of the mobile phase by switching said at least one heater system (14) on or off, and characterized in that said mobile phase heating device (3) presents a thermal inertia of less than one minute at a flow rate of 40 ml/min for a mobile phase temperature of 25Â°C at the inlet of the mobile phase heating device (3) and stabilized at 45 Â°C at the outlet of said mobile phase heating device (3).
